content.problemStatement:
In the ancient kingdom of warriors, each troop possessed unique magical powers.

You are given an array AAA of NNN integers where AiA_iAi​ represents the power of the ithi^{th}ith troop. All powers in array AAA are **distinct**.

Each troop is assigned a rank based on its power - the troop with the highest power has rank 111, and the troop with the lowest power has rank NNN. These ranks form array BBB. For example: If A=[4,5,3,7,1,9]A = [4, 5, 3, 7, 1, 9]A=[4,5,3,7,1,9], then B=[4,3,5,2,6,1]B = [4, 3, 5, 2, 6, 1]B=[4,3,5,2,6,1].

You need to remove exactly one troop from the array. When a troop is removed, the remaining troops are reranked from 111 to N−1N-1N−1 based on their powers.

Find the number of **distinct** rank arrays possible after removing exactly one troop.

In simpler terms, count how many different arrays BBB of size N−1N-1N−1 you can get after removing one troop and reranking the remaining troops.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the power of the NNN troops.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of distinct arrays BBB you can get after removing one troop from the array AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

On removing first troop, AAA becomes [8,4,1][8,4,1][8,4,1] and BBB becomes [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].
On removing second troop, AAA becomes [5,4,1][5,4,1][5,4,1] and BBB becomes [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].
On removing third troop, AAA becomes [5,8,1][5,8,1][5,8,1] and BBB becomes [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3].
On removing fourth troop, AAA becomes [5,8,4][5,8,4][5,8,4] and BBB becomes [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3].

Thus, two distinct arrays BBB are formed on removing one troop. They are [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] and [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3].
Test case 222:

On removing first troop, AAA becomes [1,4,12,7][1,4,12,7][1,4,12,7] and BBB becomes [4,3,1,2][4,3,1,2][4,3,1,2].
On removing second troop, AAA becomes [9,4,12,7][9,4,12,7][9,4,12,7] and BBB becomes [2,4,1,3][2,4,1,3][2,4,1,3].
On removing third troop, AAA becomes [9,1,12,7][9,1,12,7][9,1,12,7] and BBB becomes [2,4,1,3][2,4,1,3][2,4,1,3].
On removing fourth troop, AAA becomes [9,1,4,7][9,1,4,7][9,1,4,7] and BBB becomes [1,4,3,2][1,4,3,2][1,4,3,2].
On removing fifth troop, AAA becomes [9,1,4,12][9,1,4,12][9,1,4,12] and BBB becomes [2,4,3,1][2,4,3,1][2,4,3,1].

Thus, four distinct arrays BBB are formed on removing one troop.
